commit.message:
Security: Hardened approval workflow & fixed VM SKU (#72)

* Fix AKS cluster deployment for germanywestcentral region

- Add get_region_compatible_sku() function to provide region-specific VM SKU defaults
- Use Standard_D4s_v6 for germanywestcentral (where Standard_D4d_v4 is unavailable)
- Fall back to Standard_D4s_v5 for other regions (compatible across regions)
- Ensure default SKU is applied when --node-vm-size is not explicitly provided
- Update help text to document region-compatible SKU behavior

Fixes cluster deployment failures in germanywestcentral region by replacing
unavailable Standard_D4d_v4 with a contemporary v6-generation SKU that is
available in all target regions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* squad: Merge security review decisions (21 files, 5 critical blockers identified)

- Archived 30 entries older than 7 days (decisions.md was 300KB)
- Merged 55 unique security review findings into decisions.md
- Deduplicated 45 duplicate entries from inbox
- Identified 5 critical security blockers:
  1. Missing role/scope enforcement on approve/reject
  2. Self-approval not prevented
  3. No approver identity in audit trail
  4. workflow-engine /decide endpoint unauthenticated
  5. Unbounded input fields (Amount, Description, EmployeeId, Reason)
- Deleted all 20 inbox files after merge
- Created orchestration log and session log

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* scribe: orchestrate parallel spawn (daisy, billy, karen, rod)

- Spawn 4 agents in parallel mode for approval flow & workflow fixes
- Write orchestration logs per agent (2026-04-11T11:59:20Z)
- Write session log for orchestration tracking
- Update all agent history files with spawn context
- Archive oversized history files (daisy, billy, karen, rod >15KB)
  - daisy: 16KB → archived
  - billy: 21KB → archived
  - karen: 17KB → archived
  - rod: 47KB → archived

Metrics:
- decisions.md: 314,911 bytes (>51KB gate, no archive sections yet)
- inbox: 0 files processed
- agents spawned: 4

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* security: auth + input validation on workflow-engine /decide endpoint

- Require dapr-api-token header on POST /workflows/{instanceId}/decide
  when APP_API_TOKEN is set; fail closed in non-development environments
- Add upper-bound validation to ValidateSubmission: EmployeeId/ExpenseId/
  CorrelationId ≤ 128 chars, Description ≤ 1000 chars, Amount ≤ 1,000,000
- Add ValidateDecisionRequest: Reason ≤ 1000 chars
- Log startup warning when APP_API_TOKEN is absent

Fixes: unauthenticated /decide endpoint (critical)
Fixes: unbounded input fields (DoS/storage risk)

* Fix ProcessRunner Win32Exception handling and Windows .cmd script support (#145)

* Fix ProcessRunner Win32Exception and Windows .cmd script support

On Windows, route commands through cmd /c so .cmd/.bat scripts (like az CLI) are
resolved correctly. Also catch Win32Exception from process.Start() and wrap it as
InvalidOperationException with a descriptive message identifying the missing command.

* Fix CreateStartInfo to skip cmd wrapping for empty/null commands

On Windows, cmd /c with an empty command is treated as a no-op (exit 0),
which caused TryRunAsync_WithException_ReturnsNull to return empty string
instead of null. Skip the cmd /c wrapping when command is empty/whitespace
so the original error behavior is preserved.

* Fix ADO PR mapping: use lastMergeCommit nested object instead of flat mergeCommitId (#150)

GitCommitRef object (with commitId), not as a flat mergeCommitId string.

commit.message:
feat(loop): monitor PRs and repair legacy schema (#387)

This pull request introduces a robust system for monitoring the status
of GitHub pull requests associated with "pushed" loops, automatically
updating their state when a PR is merged. It adds a new
`pull_request_monitoring` field to the `loops` table, ensures
compatibility with legacy databases, and implements a background monitor
to periodically check and update PR status. The changes also include
comprehensive migration and regression tests to ensure reliability.

**Pull Request Monitoring Feature:**

* Added a new background service (`PushedLoopMonitor` in
`src/core/pushed-loop-monitor.ts`) that periodically checks the status
of GitHub PRs for "pushed" loops and auto-marks them as merged when
appropriate.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-3f5bcd0b49bf1883446b31a3241a3e69a0dcaee90ffac8176298fe179217e25eR1-R183)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-a2a171449d862fe29692ce031981047d7ab755ae7f84c707aef80701b3ea0c80R33)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-a2a171449d862fe29692ce031981047d7ab755ae7f84c707aef80701b3ea0c80L192-R195)
* Implemented the `probePullRequestMonitoring` function in
`src/core/pull-request-navigation.ts` to interact with the GitHub CLI,
parse PR status, and map it to a structured monitoring state.

**Database Schema and Persistence:**

* Added a new `pull_request_monitoring` column to the `loops` table
through migration `v6`, with corresponding schema repair logic in
`ensureLoopSchema` to handle legacy databases with colliding migration
versions.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-3871aec464a6e19854797223be05fecaaa3eb57958cd15631131f39391d6d3adR230-R243)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-09ba9082eabb0c34e6551b213f4c38394e452aa20fa88d33b6b33eff83ef027bL504-R511)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-c5697fcd42ef59f5948ac51fd17b76bc96b7bd5805c1e93e591765f85567a7e6R73)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-c5697fcd42ef59f5948ac51fd17b76bc96b7bd5805c1e93e591765f85567a7e6R152)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-c5697fcd42ef59f5948ac51fd17b76bc96b7bd5805c1e93e591765f85567a7e6R314-R321)
[[6]](diffhunk://#diff-a54ff182c7e8acf56acfd6e4b9c3ff41e2c41a31c9b211b2deb9df75d9a478f9R522-R531)
* Updated the `LoopState` type and helpers to persist and load the new
monitoring state.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-c16735135cfcf80ab949b21ef0a7f62f05d14b1aea0295233bed08b8c3185485R181-R183)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-c16735135cfcf80ab949b21ef0a7f62f05d14b1aea0295233bed08b8c3185485R343-R360)

**Reliability and Testing:**

* Documented the migration collision trap and required test/fix patterns
in `AGENTS.md` to guide future schema changes and prevent legacy upgrade
issues.
* Added migration and regression tests to verify the new column is added
and repaired correctly in all scenarios.

**Bug Fixes and Minor Improvements:**

* Improved idempotency of loop merging logic to avoid redundant
transitions.
* Ensured `pullRequestMonitoring` is reset on push finalization to avoid
stale state.

These changes collectively enable reliable, automated tracking of PR
status for pushed loops, with strong backward compatibility and test
coverage.

Fix: dispatch scalar function bind callback through GVL-safe executor

The bind callback was calling Ruby C API functions (rb_protect,
rb_funcall, etc.) without holding the GVL. This happened because
duckdb_query releases the GVL via rb_thread_call_without_gvl, and
DuckDB then calls the bind callback on the same thread during query
planning.

Calling Ruby without the GVL is undefined behavior that can cause
intermittent hangs or crashes, particularly on Windows where thread
scheduling differs from Linux/macOS.

Fix: route the bind callback through rbduckdb_function_executor_dispatch,
the same pattern already used by the scalar function execution callback
and all aggregate function callbacks. The dispatcher detects the calling
thread state and reacquires the GVL via rb_thread_call_with_gvl.

Also ensure the executor thread is started in set_bind, since set_bind
may be called before set_function.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
